boc_names.2: Natural and Applied Sciences and Related
boc_names.4: Education, Law, Social, Community and Government
boc_names.8: Natural Resources, Agriculture and Related Production

teer_descriptions.1: Occupations usually require a university degree
teer_descriptions.2: Occupations usually require a college diploma, apprenticeship (2+ years), or supervisory experience
teer_descriptions.3: Occupations usually require a college diploma, apprenticeship (<2 years), or >6 months OJT
teer_descriptions.4: Occupations usually require a high school diploma or several weeks of OJT
teer_descriptions.5: Occupations usually need short demonstration or OJT

caioe_legend.HH: High exposure, high complementarity (AI augments workers)

occupations: Legislative roles involve complex political judgment, public accountability, and democratic representation that AI cannot replicate.
Senior managers - financial, communications and other business services
Senior managers increasingly use AI for strategic analytics and forecasting, but stakeholder relationships and final judgment remain human.
Senior managers - health, education, social and community services and membership organizations
Leadership in health, education and social services involves human relationship management alongside AI-augmentable analytical work.
Senior managers - trade, broadcasting and other services, n.e.c.
Trade and broadcasting senior managers face AI in content and market analytics but strategic direction remains human.
Senior managers - construction, transportation, production and utilities
Construction and production management involves physical oversight and human coordination alongside AI-assisted planning.
Government senior managers increasingly rely on AI for policy analysis but accountability and political judgment are human.
Financial management increasingly AI-augmented for forecasting, risk and compliance, but capital allocation decisions remain human.
HR management involves talent strategy and culture work that is human-centric alongside AI-automatable administrative functions.
Purchasing management uses AI for spend analytics and supplier evaluation but strategic negotiations remain human.
Administrative services management increasingly AI-assisted for workflow optimization.
Insurance, real estate and financial brokerage managers
Insurance and real estate management involves client relationships and regulatory judgment alongside AI-assisted risk analysis.
Marketing and advertising management heavily transformed by AI-driven campaign analytics, content generation, and targeting.
Business services management uses AI for process optimization but client relationships remain human.
Financial auditing and accounting are prime AI targets: reconciliation, compliance checks, and financial reporting are highly automatable.
Financial analysis — modeling, forecasting, report writing — is directly in the path of AI tools that already outperform human analysts on structured tasks.
Securities agents, investment dealers and brokers
Securities trading and investment advice increasingly AI-driven; relationship-based advisory retains human value.
Financial officer roles involving reporting, compliance and regulatory filings are highly structured and automatable.
HR professionals face AI automation in screening and onboarding; strategic talent and culture work remains human.
Professional occupations in business management consulting
Management consulting involves research, analysis, and report writing that are all highly AI-amenable; client persuasion is human.
Business development officers and market researchers and analysts
Market research and business development involve data analysis (AI-high) and relationship building (AI-low).
Purchasing involves negotiation and supplier relationships alongside automatable spend analysis.
Event planning requires in-person logistics and vendor relationships alongside AI-automatable scheduling and budgeting.
Administrative supervision involves team management and exception handling that is human-centric.
Property administration mixes physical site management with AI-automatable tenant communications and documentation.
Court clerks and related court services occupations
Court clerks work within strict procedural requirements; document management is automatable but procedural oversight requires human judgment.
Administrative assistant tasks — scheduling, email drafting, document management — are being automated by AI tools.
Legal administrative work involving document preparation and deadline tracking is a direct AI automation target.
Medical admin has regulatory complexity limiting automation, but appointment scheduling and billing coding are increasingly automated.
Executive assistants increasingly use AI tools; complex scheduling, travel logistics, and senior stakeholder management retain human value.
Bookkeeping and transactional accounting are among the most directly displaced roles; AI tools like QuickBooks AI are rapidly automating core tasks.
Insurance claims examination involves structured data review and rule application that is highly automatable.
Collections and credit decisions are increasingly rule-based and automated.
Administrative officers coordinate complex workflows and exceptions; some automation but human judgment required.
HR recruitment increasingly uses AI for resume screening and candidate matching; human judgment still required for final decisions.
Payroll calculation, tax compliance, and reporting are highly structured and automatable.
Records management — classification, retention scheduling, retrieval — is a prime AI and automation target.
General office support involving data entry, filing, and routine correspondence is a core automation target.
Data entry is the single most directly displaced occupation — AI handles structured data capture faster and more accurately than humans.
Receptionists require human social presence and contextual judgment; scheduling and call routing are increasingly automated.
Physical delivery requires in-person presence; autonomous vehicle delivery is developing but full deployment is years away.
Mail sorting is increasingly automated; delivery remains physical but volumes are declining.
Office support tasks are increasingly automated by AI tools; physical tasks limit full automation.
IT management requires technical judgment and people leadership; AI accelerates infrastructure but doesn't replace strategic architecture decisions.
Architecture and science management involves technical expertise and project oversight that is partially AI-augmented.
Engineering management requires physical site oversight and cross-disciplinary judgment alongside AI-assisted planning tools.
Physics research increasingly uses ML for data analysis and simulation, but hypothesis formation and experimental design remain human.
AI accelerates molecular discovery and literature synthesis, but experimental chemistry requires physical laboratory work.
AI enhances seismic interpretation and resource modeling; extensive fieldwork remains human.
Weather modeling increasingly AI-driven; atmospheric scientists focus on interpretation and communication.
Other professional occupations in physical sciences
Biological research is AI-augmented in data analysis; wet-lab and field work remains human-intensive.
Forestry professionals require physical field assessment; data analysis increasingly automated.
Agricultural consulting involves farm visits and practical advice alongside AI-assisted data analysis.
Other professional occupations in life sciences
Civil engineering increasingly AI-assisted in design and simulation; site supervision and professional judgment remain human.
Mechanical design simulation and optimization increasingly AI-driven; manufacturing oversight remains human.
Electrical engineering involves circuit simulation and system design that is heavily AI-augmented.
Chemical process design and simulation are AI-enhanced; safety-critical oversight remains human.
Industrial engineering process optimization is highly AI-amenable; implementation requires human coordination.
Materials engineering involves laboratory work and failure analysis alongside AI-assisted simulation.
Mining engineering requires physical site expertise and safety management alongside AI-assisted modeling.
Geological engineering integrates field data with AI-assisted subsurface modeling.
Petroleum engineering uses AI for reservoir modeling; field operations and safety management are human.
Aerospace engineering increasingly uses AI for design optimization and simulation.
Computer engineers (except software engineers and designers)
Computer hardware engineering increasingly AI-assisted in design verification and optimization.
AI coding assistants are transforming software engineering — productivity multiplied, with emerging headcount pressure on junior developers.
Architectural design increasingly AI-assisted; professional judgment, client engagement, and site oversight remain human.
Landscape architecture involves spatial design and environmental assessment that is AI-augmented but not automated.
Naval architecture involves specialized structural design with increasing AI simulation support.
Urban planning involves data analysis (AI-high) and public engagement and political negotiation (AI-low).
Land surveying involves physical measurement and verification; office processing increasingly automated.
Statistical modeling is core to AI itself; actuarial and statistical analysis roles are significantly AI-augmented.
Data scientists are increasingly AI-augmented, with AI automating exploratory analysis and model prototyping.
Systems analysis and requirements documentation are highly AI-augmented; stakeholder management remains human.
Business systems work — requirements gathering, process mapping, documentation — is significantly AI-automatable.
Database design, query optimization, and schema documentation increasingly AI-assisted.
AI coding copilots are fundamentally changing software development; junior developer roles are most at risk.
Web design increasingly AI-assisted for layout, prototyping, and content generation.
Web development heavily AI-augmented; low-complexity sites increasingly AI-generated without human developers.
Desktop publishing operators and related occupations
Cybersecurity requires adaptive human expertise to respond to AI-enabled attacks while using AI for defense.
Civil engineering technologists support design and construction; field inspection remains human.
Mechanical technologists operate and maintain equipment with increasing AI-assisted diagnostics.
Industrial engineering and manufacturing technologists and technicians
Industrial engineering technologists increasingly use AI for process optimization and monitoring.
Construction estimating involves quantity takeoff and cost modeling that is increasingly AI-assisted.
Electrical and electronics engineering technologists and technicians
Electrical technologists test and document systems; AI accelerates fault diagnosis.
Electronic service technicians (household and business equipment)
Household and business equipment repair is increasingly complex but AI-assisted diagnostics are growing.
Industrial instrument work requires physical calibration and in-field expertise.
Architectural technologists produce drawings increasingly with AI-assisted CAD tools.
Drafting work is being transformed by AI-assisted CAD; 3D modeling and simulation increasingly automated.
Geomatics and meteorology technicians and technologists
Geomatics technicians collect field data; GIS analysis is increasingly AI-augmented.
Life sciences technicians and related occupations (except medical)
Life sciences technicians perform laboratory procedures; data analysis increasingly automated.
Food and fish inspection requires physical examination and sensory assessment.
Other technical occupations in applied sciences, n.e.c.
Network technicians increasingly use AI-assisted monitoring and troubleshooting tools.
User support is being transformed by AI chatbots and self-service tools; complex cases still require human technicians.
Software testing increasingly automated by AI; test design and edge-case identification remain human.
Healthcare management involves AI-assisted operational optimization alongside human leadership of clinical teams.
Specialists in clinical and laboratory medicine
AI diagnostic tools are advancing rapidly in radiology and pathology, but specialist physicians retain critical judgment, patient communication, and complex case management.
Surgeons are supported by robotic systems but remain central to all complex procedures and intraoperative judgment.
AI supports diagnostics and documentation; patient relationships and complex clinical judgment are irreplaceable.
Veterinary medicine involves physical examination and hands-on procedures alongside AI-assisted diagnostics.
Other professional occupations in health diagnosing
Dental procedures are highly physical; AI assists with diagnostic imaging but hands-on work is irreplaceable.
AI eye screening is advancing; optometrists retain comprehensive examination and prescription judgment.
Therapeutic communication and individualized speech and hearing treatment are human-intensive.
Drug interaction checking is increasingly automated; patient counseling and complex medication management remain human.
Personalized nutrition planning is AI-assisted; patient motivation, clinical complexity and comorbidities require human expertise.
Physical therapy involves hands-on assessment, manual therapy, and patient motivation that AI cannot replicate.
Occupational therapy requires in-person adaptive assessment and functional rehabilitation planning.
Kinesiologists and other professional occupations in therapy and assessment
Kinesiology involves physical assessment and exercise prescription alongside AI-assisted data analysis.
Therapeutic relationships and mental health treatment require deep human connection; AI support tools are supplementary.
Chiropractic care involves physical manipulation and patient assessment that is inherently hands-on.
Other professional occupations in health diagnosing, n.e.c.
Registered nurses and registered psychiatric nurses
Clinical judgment, patient advocacy, and physical care remain human-critical; AI assists monitoring and documentation.
Hands-on patient care limits automation; LPN roles increasingly supported by AI-assisted charting tools.
Nurse practitioners retain autonomous clinical practice; AI assists decision support and documentation.
Nursing coordination involves team management and patient flow optimization alongside AI-assisted scheduling.
Laboratory technology is semi-automated; quality assurance and complex sample handling require skilled technologists.
Radiation technology involves precise image acquisition; AI increasingly assists interpretation but technologists operate equipment.
Skilled ultrasound transducer operation requires human expertise; AI assists interpretation.
Cardiology technologists and electrophysiological diagnostic technologists, n.e.c.
Cardiology technologists perform technical procedures requiring precision; AI assists interpretation.
Dental hygiene procedures are hands-on and patient-facing; some documentation AI-assisted.
Respiratory therapists, clinical perfusionists and cardiopulmonary technologists
Respiratory therapy involves complex ventilator management and patient assessment; AI monitors parameters.
Occupational therapy and physiotherapy support occupations
Physiotherapy and OT support involves hands-on exercise and daily living assistance.
Other technical occupations in therapy and assessment
Denturists perform hands-on dental appliance fabrication and fitting.
Pharmacy dispensing is increasingly automated; technician roles shift toward verification and patient interaction.
Medical laboratory assistants and related technical occupations
Laboratory automation is advancing; sample handling and quality control still require human technicians.
Dental assistants and dental laboratory assistants
Dental assisting involves chairside patient care and tray preparation that is hands-on.
Opticians fit and dispense eyewear; some automation in lens processing but patient fitting remains human.
Other assisting occupations in support of health services
Paramedics provide emergency care under acute time pressure; AI supports dispatch but cannot replace field response.
Natural healing practitioners provide individualized hands-on and counselling-based care.
Other technical occupations in health, n.e.c.
Nurse aides, orderlies and patient service associates
Nurse aides provide physical care, hygiene assistance, and patient comfort — inherently human work.
Home health care involves physical assistance, companionship, and safety monitoring for vulnerable clients.
Funeral directors, embalmers and related occupations
Funeral directors provide grief support and physical preparation of the deceased requiring profound human care.
Other assisting occupations in support of health services
Government managers - health and social policy development and program administration
Government health and social policy managers use AI for evidence synthesis; policy judgment and stakeholder relations are human.
Government managers - economic analysis, policy development and program administration
Economic policy analysis increasingly AI-augmented; political judgment and stakeholder engagement are human.
Government managers - education policy development and program administration
Education policy development involves research and stakeholder consultation alongside AI-assisted analysis.
Public administration management involves legislative compliance and political accountability that is human-centric.
Managers in social, community and correctional services
Social services management involves community relationships and crisis response alongside AI-assisted case management.
Judicial reasoning, constitutional interpretation, and courtroom management require unimpeachable human accountability.
Legal research and document review are directly AI-disrupted; court advocacy, complex strategy, and client trust remain human.
Research and teaching involve deep expertise and mentorship; AI assists research synthesis and grading.
Research assistance and course facilitation are AI-augmented; human supervision and mentorship retain value.
Social work involves physical home visits, trust-building, trauma-informed care, and crisis intervention that are inherently human.
Family, marriage and other related counsellors
Family counselling requires therapeutic presence and emotional attunement that AI cannot replicate.
Probation and parole officers and related occupations
Probation officers conduct in-person supervision and rehabilitation support alongside AI-assisted risk assessment tools.
Employment counselling involves career assessment and coaching that is semi-AI-automatable.
Other professional occupations in social science, n.e.c.
Economists and economic policy researchers and analysts
Economic modeling and data analysis are highly AI-amenable; judgment on policy implications and communication remain human.
Policy researchers, consultants and program officers
Policy research and analysis are significantly AI-augmented; stakeholder engagement and advocacy remain human.
Other professional occupations in social science
Secondary school teaching involves classroom management, student relationships, and adaptive instruction — deeply human work.
Elementary and kindergarten teaching requires developmental attunement, physical presence, and emotional connection.
Special education requires intensive human relationships and highly adaptive individualized support.
Other teachers in elementary and secondary schools
Teaching persons with disabilities requires physical and therapeutic assistance and sustained human relationship.
Vocational instruction requires physical demonstration, hands-on supervision, and industry expertise.
Spiritual leadership, pastoral care, and community ritual are deeply human vocations.
Social policy researchers, consultants and program officers
Early childhood education requires physical care, emotional attunement, and developmental responsiveness.
Visiting homemakers, housekeepers and related occupations
Home-based domestic assistance and elder companionship are inherently human services.
Legal document preparation, research, and drafting are direct AI targets; AI paralegal tools are already transforming this role.
Community service work involves in-person client support and crisis response that is human-centric.
Corrections work involves physical supervision and rehabilitation support in custodial settings.
Bylaw enforcement and other regulatory officers, n.e.c.
Bylaw enforcement involves physical inspection and discretionary judgment in community settings.
Elementary and secondary school teacher assistants
Teacher assistants provide hands-on support, behaviour management, and physical assistance in classrooms.
Community support work involves in-person case management, crisis response, and practical assistance.
Other paraprofessional occupations in law, education
Managers - publishing, motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts
Publishing and media management requires creative strategy and industry judgment alongside AI-assisted analytics.
Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
Recreation program management involves community relationships and physical program delivery.
Managers in libraries, archives, museums and art galleries
Library and museum management involves curatorial judgment and community programming.
Information curation and research assistance are AI-augmented; community programming and specialized reference remain human.
Archival appraisal and provenance research require specialized expertise; classification increasingly AI-assisted.
Art conservation requires physical expertise and material knowledge; curatorial judgment has aesthetic and cultural dimensions.
AI writing assistants and content generation are rapidly transforming editorial roles; human judgment on voice and meaning still valued.
Generative AI directly competes with writing tasks; distinctive voice, originality, and deep expertise remain human advantages.
Technical documentation is highly structured and increasingly AI-generated; output quality of AI tools is already competitive.
Journalism involves information gathering, verification, and narrative writing — AI handles structured reporting well; investigative work less so.
Machine translation quality has surged; interpreting and nuanced literary/legal translation still value human expertise.
Producers, directors, choreographers and related occupations
Creative direction and production management are human; AI assists in pre-production and post-production editing.
Musical composition involves artistic vision and emotional expression; AI tools are assistive not replacements for original creative artists.
AI music generation is growing but live performance, session work, and personal artistry retain human value.
Dance is an embodied physical art form; performance is irreducibly human.
Acting involves emotional authenticity and physical performance; AI cannot replicate lived human experience.
Painters, sculptors and other visual artists
Visual arts practice involves conceptual development and physical creation; AI tools assist but don't replace artistic vision.
Patternmakers - textile, leather and fur products
AI photo generation and editing are advancing; creative event and portrait photography and photojournalism retain human value.
Creative cinematography and camera operation require artistic judgment and physical presence.
AI image generation is directly impacting illustration and graphic design; distinctive style and client relationships still valued.
Interior design involves spatial judgment, client empathy, and project management alongside AI visualization tools.
Theatre, fashion, exhibit and other creative designers
Creative design for theatre and fashion requires physical material knowledge and conceptual vision.
Industrial design involves physical prototyping and user research alongside AI-assisted form generation.
Broadcasting tasks are increasingly automated; live announcing and personality-driven programming retain value.
Audio recording engineering requires trained ears and technical judgment in live and studio settings.
Elite athletic performance is quintessentially human and physical.
Coaching involves human relationships, motivational skill, and embodied sport knowledge.
Officiating requires real-time physical presence and contextual judgment.
Recreation, sports and fitness program and service directors
Recreation, sports and fitness program and service workers
In-person recreation and fitness program delivery is community and human-centered.
Motion pictures, broadcasting and performing arts support
Library and archive workers and technicians
Library and archive technicians assist with information access; cataloguing increasingly automated.
Conservation and fishery officers conduct physical field enforcement and environmental assessment.
Retail management involves floor supervision, staff management, and customer experience that is human-centric.
Managers in food service and accommodation
Food service management involves physical operations, staff supervision, and customer experience.
Managers in customer and personal services, n.e.c.
Customer service management requires team leadership and complaint resolution in service environments.
Real estate involves property matching and market analysis (AI-assisted) plus client relationships and negotiation (human).
Insurance sales involves policy analysis (AI-assisted) and client needs assessment and relationship management (human).
Technical sales specialists in wholesale trade
Technical sales requires product expertise and client relationship alongside AI-assisted lead scoring.
Non-technical sales representatives in wholesale trade
Wholesale sales involves relationship management and product knowledge alongside AI-assisted territory planning.
Culinary craft, menu creativity, and kitchen leadership are deeply human and physical.
Retail buying involves demand forecasting (AI-high) and vendor negotiation and trend judgment (human).
Food service supervision requires physical presence, team management, and quality control.
Housekeeping management involves physical operations and staff supervision in accommodation settings.
Accommodation, travel, tourism and related services supervisors
Accommodation and travel supervision involves guest relations and physical service delivery.
Customer service supervision increasingly AI-assisted; complex escalations require human judgment.
Cooking is physically complex; AI menus and robotic cooking exist at scale but skilled cooks remain essential.
Artisan baking remains human; industrial baking increasingly automated.
Butchers and meat cutters - retail and wholesale
Meat cutting is a skilled manual trade; robotic butchering has limited deployment.
Bartending involves skilled drink preparation and social interaction; robot bartenders are novelty not mainstream.
Robo-advisors are growing rapidly; complex financial planning and trust relationships retain human value for high-net-worth clients.
AI travel booking and planning tools are displacing travel agents; complex itineraries still benefit from human expertise.
Flight attendants provide in-person safety and service that requires human judgment and adaptability.
Airline ticketing increasingly automated; service agents handle complex exceptions and customer relations.
Ground and water transport ticket agents
Contact centre and customer information service agents
Contact centre work is being transformed by AI chatbots and voice AI; complex and emotional calls retain human handling.
Food and beverage service involves hospitality and human connection that resists full automation.
Food counter attendants, kitchen helpers and related support occupations
Fast food counter automation is advancing; most counter service still human but self-ordering kiosks are reducing headcount.
Security guards and related security service occupations
Physical security and situational judgment require human presence; monitoring increasingly augmented by AI.
Esthetic services require skilled touch and personal interaction; highly resistant to automation.
Haircutting and styling are physically intimate and highly personalized; essentially immune to current AI.
Dry cleaning, laundry and related occupations
Industrial laundry is increasingly automated; residential dry cleaning still human.
In-store retail requires human assistance and product knowledge; e-commerce automation growing.
Self-checkout and automated payment systems are directly displacing cashier roles.
In-home child care is an irreducibly human relationship of trust and developmental care.
Home support workers, housekeepers and related occupations
Personal care and companionship for elderly and disabled people are deeply human vocations.
Pet groomers and animal care workers
Pet grooming involves skilled physical handling and animal rapport.
Construction management requires physical site oversight and safety management alongside AI-assisted project scheduling.
Facility management involves physical inspections and maintenance coordination.
Transportation management involves fleet operations and logistics planning increasingly AI-augmented.
Electrical installation is physically complex and variable; diagnostics increasingly digital but installation remains human.
Electrical power line and cable workers
High-voltage power line work requires trained human workers in hazardous conditions.
Telecommunications infrastructure installation is a physical trade with high variability.
Cable television service and maintenance technicians
Electrical and electronics repair requires physical diagnostic skill in varied equipment types.
Other electricians and electrical workers, n.e.c.
Plumbing involves physically complex problem-solving in varied and unpredictable environments.
Steamfitters, pipefitters and sprinkler system installers
Pipefitting requires physical skill and spatial problem-solving in industrial environments.
Gas fitting requires licensed physical work in safety-critical residential and commercial settings.
Carpentry requires physical skill and adaptability in highly variable construction environments.
Cabinet making combines skilled manual craft with increasing CNC augmentation.
Insulation installation requires hands-on physical work in confined and variable spaces.
Masonry requires physical skill and judgment; bricklaying robots are emerging but deployment is limited.
Plasterers, drywall installers and finishers and lathers
Drywall installation involves physical precision in construction environments.
Tile setting requires skilled physical installation with material and design judgment.
Painting and decorating is physically skilled work in residential and commercial settings.
Floor covering installation requires physical precision and material expertise.
Roofing is physically demanding, safety-critical, and highly resistant to automation.
Glazing requires precision physical installation of glass in construction environments.
Concrete finishing requires physical skill and time-sensitive judgment during curing.
Machinists and machining and tooling inspectors
CNC machining is highly automated; setup, programming, and tooling inspection require skilled operators.
Tool and die making is a precision craft with increasing CNC augmentation.
Ironwork involves structural installation requiring physical skill and safety judgment.
Sheet metal work involves fabrication and installation in variable environments.
Boilermaking involves fabrication and maintenance of high-pressure vessels.
Automated welding robots grow in manufacturing; complex and custom welding remains skilled human work.
Heating, refrigeration and air conditioning mechanics
HVAC installation and repair requires physical presence in residential and commercial environments.
Heavy equipment repair requires physical expertise and on-site diagnostic work in challenging environments.
Auto body repair requires physical skill in collision restoration and finishing.
Motor vehicle mechanics, technicians and diagnostic technicians
Vehicle diagnostics increasingly AI-assisted; physical repair and calibration remain human.
Aircraft maintenance requires precise physical inspection and repair under strict regulatory requirements.
Air pilots, flight engineers and flying instructors
Autopilot has long augmented pilots; safety regulations and public trust require human pilots for commercial aviation.
Air traffic controllers and related occupations
Air traffic control requires safety-critical human oversight; AI provides decision support but not autonomous control.
Deck officers navigate vessels with increasing automation support but maintain command responsibility.
Railway traffic controllers and marine traffic regulators
Railway traffic control uses increasing automation; safety rules maintain human oversight roles.
Railway and yard locomotive engineers and conductors
Locomotive engineers operate trains with increasing automation support; safety-critical human oversight maintained.
Railway conductors coordinate train operations; automation is increasing but human oversight remains required.
Crane operation requires skilled real-time spatial judgment in dynamic construction environments.
Drillers and blasters - surface mining, quarrying and construction
Surface drilling and blasting requires physical expertise and safety management.
Oil and gas well drillers, servicers, testers and related workers
Oil and gas well drilling and servicing require physical expertise in remote and hazardous conditions.
Machine tool operation increasingly automated; operator oversight and setup still require human skill.
Woodworking machine operation involves setup and quality monitoring alongside automated production.
Textile, fabric, fur and leather cutters
Local transit driving is semi-automated; full autonomy is developing but regulatory and operational barriers remain.
Long-haul autonomous trucks are developing; most Canadian trucking still requires human drivers for complex routes.
Bus drivers, subway operators and other transit operators
Transit bus driving requires human judgment in urban environments; autonomous transit in development.
Heavy equipment operation requires skilled physical control in variable terrain environments.
Longshore work involves physical cargo handling in variable port environments.
Material handling increasingly automated in warehouses; pick-and-place robots growing but human handlers still dominant.
Elevator installation requires physical precision and electrical expertise.
Residential and commercial installers and servicers
Residential and commercial installation services require physical presence and problem-solving.
Waterworks and gas maintenance requires physical inspection and repair in municipal infrastructure.
Taxi and limousine drivers and chauffeurs
Ride-hailing has transformed this sector; fully autonomous vehicles are emerging but widespread deployment is years away.
Delivery service drivers and couriers, except postal
Last-mile delivery involves variable environments; autonomous delivery is developing but human couriers still dominant.
Shipping and receiving involves physical goods handling with increasing automation in large facilities.
Warehouse management involves inventory management increasingly augmented by AI-assisted WMS systems.
Storekeeping involves physical inventory management and goods handling.
Other equipment operators and related workers
Construction labour involves physical tasks requiring on-site adaptability.
Public works maintenance involves physical infrastructure upkeep in variable environments.
Transport labour involves physical goods handling and vehicle support.
Managers in natural resources production and fishing
Natural resource management involves field operations and regulatory compliance alongside AI-assisted production optimization.
Farm management increasingly uses precision agriculture AI tools; physical operations and land expertise remain human.
Forestry supervision requires physical on-site management and safety oversight.
Mine supervision requires physical site management in safety-critical environments.
Supervisors, oil and gas drilling and service
Oil and gas drilling supervision involves safety management in remote conditions.
Farm supervision requires physical on-site management and crop/livestock expertise.
Horticultural supervision involves physical plant management and agronomic decision-making.
Aquaculture supervision involves physical water environment management and biological monitoring.
Logging is physically demanding work in variable forest terrain; limited automation deployment.
Silviculture workers perform physical tree planting and forest tending tasks.
Farm work is physically demanding; robotic harvesting is growing but most agricultural work remains human.
Nursery and greenhouse work involves physical plant care and environmental monitoring.
Trapping and hunting are traditional land-based occupations deeply tied to physical and ecological knowledge.
Commercial fishing requires physical skill and environmental adaptability in variable marine conditions.
Aquaculture operations increasingly use sensor monitoring and AI-assisted feeding systems.
Underground miners and mine service workers
Underground mining requires skilled workers in safety-critical confined environments.
Oil and gas well drilling workers and services operators
Drilling and well servicing require specialized physical expertise in remote field conditions.
Explosives work requires licensed physical expertise in safety-critical environments.
Oil and gas well drilling workers
Surface drilling requires physical operation and safety management.
Manufacturing management requires physical plant oversight and operations leadership alongside AI-assisted production optimization.
Processing supervision requires physical presence and quality management.
Assembly supervision involves physical quality oversight and team management on production lines.
Utility supervision requires physical operations management and safety oversight.
Central control and process operators, petroleum, gas and chemical processing
Process control operators monitor increasingly automated plants; critical interventions require skilled humans.
Power engineers and power systems operators
Power engineers operate critical infrastructure with increasing automation; human oversight required.
Water and waste treatment plant operators
Water treatment plant operators monitor automated systems and make treatment adjustments.
Food processing supervision involves physical quality control and staff management.
Textile processing supervision involves physical operations and quality management.
Machine operators, mineral and metal products processing
Machine operation is increasingly automated; setup and oversight still require human operators.
Plastics and rubber processing increasingly automated in larger plants.
Chemical plant operation requires physical process monitoring and safety management.
Sawmill machine operators and related workers
Sawmill operations are highly mechanized; operators manage automated systems and handle exceptions.
Textile and fabric machine operators and related workers
Textile manufacturing is increasingly automated in large-scale production.
Industrial sewing involves skilled machine operation; automation is advancing in standardized garment production.
Furniture and fixture assemblers and inspectors
Other wood products assemblers and related workers
Assemblers, fabricators and inspectors, industrial electrical and electronic equipment
Electronic equipment assembly increasingly uses robotic systems; human assemblers handle complex components.
Motor vehicle assemblers, inspectors and testers
Auto assembly is heavily robotized; human workers handle complex assembly and quality tasks.
Electronics assemblers, fabricators, inspectors and testers
Electronics assembly increasingly automated; skilled human assembly still needed for complex boards.
Assemblers and inspectors, industrial electrical motors
Other products assemblers, finishers and inspectors
Products assembly varies widely; automation penetration depends on product complexity.
Industrial butchers and meat cutters, poultry preparers and related workers
Meat processing has significant automation; skilled cutting still requires human precision for complex cuts.
Artisan baking remains human; industrial baking production is increasingly automated.
Packaging automation is widespread; human packers handle variable or fragile products.
Textile, cut and sew and related occupations
Textile cutting and sewing increasingly automated in industrial production.
Print production is highly automated; prepress and specialty printing retain skilled operators.
Camera, platemaking and related occupations in printing
Electrical power line and telecommunications workers
Power line maintenance requires physical work in safety-critical high-voltage environments.
Waterworks and gas maintenance requires physical inspection and repair.
Labourers in mineral and metal products processing and utilities
Processing labourers perform repetitive tasks increasingly subject to automation in large plants.
Labourers in chemical products processing and utilities
Chemical processing labour involves repetitive tasks in increasingly automated facilities.
Labourers in wood, pulp and paper processing
Wood and pulp processing labour involves physical tasks in semi-automated facilities.
Labourers in rubber and plastic products manufacturing
Rubber and plastics labour involves repetitive processing tasks with growing automation.
Labourers in food, beverage and tobacco processing
Food processing labour involves repetitive tasks; automation is advancing in large-scale facilities.
Labourers in fish and seafood processing
Fish and seafood processing requires physical dexterity handling variable marine products.
Textile processing labour is increasingly automated in industrial settings.
Metal fabrication labour involves physical tasks with growing robotic assistance.
Electronics manufacturing labour increasingly performed by automated assembly systems.
Labourers in motor vehicle and transport equipment manufacturing
Motor vehicle manufacturing labour works alongside increasing robotics in assembly plants.
Other labourers in processing, manufacturing and utilities
Other manufacturing labour includes a range of tasks with varying automation exposure.
